jokes.text: Well, that's not very helpful, John. You're the only one using the sign-out sheet.
jokes.setup: Johnny's elaborate sign-out system
jokes.punchline: Moira pointing out he's the only one participating in his own system
jokes.explanation: Clean sarcastic observation that deflates Johnny's bureaucratic pretensions

jokes.text: Because you're the only one using that sign-out sheet.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking why David's name isn't on the sheet
jokes.punchline: David echoing Moira's exact point
jokes.explanation: Repetition callback emphasizing how obvious the point is

jokes.text: I can't take four hours in the truck with Roland and Lynyrd Skynyrd.
jokes.setup: Roland offering to drive Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's horror at Roland's music taste
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast between sophisticated Moira and blue-collar Roland, economically stated

jokes.text: If airplane safety videos have taught me anything, David, it's that a mother puts her own mask on first.
jokes.setup: David's appeal to parental duty
jokes.punchline: Moira using airplane safety metaphor to justify selfishness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected metaphor that's both logical and absurd, perfectly delivered with Moira's pretentious tone

jokes.text: Ah, see, and that's precisely why we have the sign-out sheet.
jokes.setup: Family car argument
jokes.punchline: Johnny thinking his useless system validates this chaos
jokes.explanation: Johnny's delusion that his bureaucracy would help this situation

jokes.text: Under purpose of trip he writes 'driving.'
jokes.setup: Johnny's complaint about system abuse
jokes.punchline: David's hilariously literal and unhelpful form completion
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of David's sarcastic compliance with Johnny's bureaucracy

jokes.text: I want to see the look on your face when you walk into your first regional association of municipalities conference.
jokes.setup: Roland building up the conference
jokes.punchline: The absurd grandiosity he attributes to a municipal conference
jokes.explanation: Roland's excitement about mundane municipal affairs shows his small-town perspective

jokes.text: I won't wear anything with an adhesive backing.
jokes.setup: Roland offering name tags
jokes.punchline: Moira's pretentious refusal based on adhesive quality
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Moira's absurd standards and pretentious vocabulary

jokes.text: You mean the one serving drinks to the other people?
jokes.setup: Roland pointing out the well-dressed mayor
jokes.punchline: Moira realizing he's pointing to catering staff
jokes.explanation: Simple mistaken identity joke with Moira's dry delivery

jokes.text: Well, now you've identified the catering staff.
jokes.setup: Roland trying again to identify the mayor
jokes.punchline: Moira pointing out he's still wrong
jokes.explanation: Escalates the previous joke, showing Roland still doesn't know who's important

jokes.text: I mean, it's really kinda scary that we're all in here under one roof.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining the importance of municipal officials
jokes.punchline: His melodramatic fear of municipal workers gathering
jokes.explanation: Roland's inflated sense of municipal drama and importance

jokes.text: If there is anyone at this fabulous little confab who know how to work a room of fragile egos, it's me.
jokes.setup: Roland asking for Moira's help with the pitch
jokes.punchline: Moira's condescending confidence about managing egos
jokes.explanation: Moira's pretentious vocabulary ('confab') and unconscious irony about fragile egos

jokes.text: I once hosted the non-televised portion of the People Choice Awards.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her qualification for working rooms
jokes.punchline: Her impressive credential is actually the unimportant part of an award show
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Moira's delusional self-importance - bragging about the least important part of an award show

jokes.text: That's like 53 pounds.
jokes.setup: David asking Alexis to get the box of hand cream
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dramatic complaint about the weight
jokes.explanation: Alexis's melodramatic reaction to basic physical labor

jokes.text: I'm currently studying business and I'm David's sister and life coach so.
jokes.setup: Alexis introducing herself to Patrick
jokes.punchline: Her self-appointed role as David's life coach
jokes.explanation: Alexis's delusional confidence in appointing herself David's life coach

jokes.text: I'm sorry if my hands are too soft. I've just been sampling a lot of product.
jokes.setup: Alexis shaking Patrick's hand
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt to turn product sampling into a flirtation point
jokes.explanation: Alexis's awkward flirting attempts show her character's social obliviousness

jokes.text: Well, I was simply channeled someone who cares, but if you insist on referring to me as the linchpin. I'll take that.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about Moira's successful speech
jokes.punchline: Moira accepting a title the reporter never offered
jokes.explanation: Moira's ego makes her hear compliments that weren't given, with her typical pretentious phrasing

jokes.text: Yes, I'll I'm the ingenue here. A total babe in the political woods.
jokes.setup: Roland saying it's Moira's first RAMC
jokes.punchline: Moira's theatrical description of herself as a political newcomer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant theatrical language - 'ingenue' and 'babe in the woods' showing Moira's dramatic self-perception

jokes.text: They have a special RAMC cocktail. It's a sparkling white Russian. The trick is to get it down before it curdles.
jokes.setup: Roland describing the diamond club
jokes.punchline: The disgusting-sounding signature cocktail
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful small-town attempt at sophistication with a gross drink description

jokes.text: Uh, that is actually cat hair. There's a Himalayan breeder up the street that knits them for us.
jokes.setup: Characters commenting on the softness of fabric
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact explanation about cat hair products
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and slightly disturbing product detail delivered with David's deadpan tone

jokes.text: Because I was just thinking that this frame is a little too corporate for my brand.
jokes.setup: Patrick saying the frames all come that way
jokes.punchline: David's relief that he can criticize the frame without insulting Patrick
jokes.explanation: David's brand obsession and slight social awkwardness around Patrick

jokes.text: Okay, well I flattened out the lip balm. So no one's gonna notice.
jokes.setup: David saying they need to sell products, not sample them
jokes.punchline: Alexis's logic that flattening product somehow hides the theft
jokes.explanation: Alexis's ridiculous attempt to cover up her product sampling

jokes.text: Actually, the textbooks now say eighteen months.
jokes.setup: Patrick saying businesses should survive a year without profit
jokes.punchline: Alexis 'correcting' him with worse news
jokes.explanation: Alexis's eagerness to show off her business knowledge makes things worse

jokes.text: Well, I don't have my textbook on me.
jokes.setup: David asking what textbooks say about his business model
jokes.punchline: Alexis's literal response to his sarcastic question
jokes.explanation: Alexis missing David's sarcasm completely

jokes.text: The cheering and accolades are drowning out your gentle voice.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to talk to drunk Moira on the phone
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic description of background noise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira theatricality - turning mundane party noise into epic triumph

jokes.text: That's my man, Johnny!
jokes.setup: Roland taking over Moira's phone call
jokes.punchline: Roland's inappropriate familiarity with Johnny
jokes.explanation: Roland's drunk overfamiliarity creating awkwardness

jokes.text: Goodbye, phone call!
jokes.setup: Moira ending the phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Her theatrical farewell to the phone itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Moira moment - treating the phone call as a person to bid farewell

jokes.text: Roland! What are you doing in my room?
jokes.setup: Moira waking up confused
jokes.punchline: Discovering Roland in what she thinks is her room
jokes.explanation: Setup for the major misunderstanding that drives the episode's plot

jokes.text: Well, what the fuck are you doing in your room?
jokes.setup: Roland saying it's his room
jokes.punchline: Moira's confused anger at Roland being in his own room
jokes.explanation: Moira's confusion escalating as she realizes her mistake

jokes.text: Are you naked under there?
jokes.setup: Moira realizing she spent the night with Roland
jokes.punchline: Her horror at the possibility of his nudity
jokes.explanation: Escalating Moira's horror as she pieces together what happened

jokes.text: Honey, what I do in the privacy of my sheets is my own business.
jokes.setup: Moira asking if he's naked
jokes.punchline: Roland's matter-of-fact defense of sleeping nude
jokes.explanation: Roland's casual attitude about his nudity contrasts with Moira's horror

jokes.text: Roland and Moira, sleeping together.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining Johnny's tough morning
jokes.punchline: The bombshell news delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - dropping the bombshell casually while Johnny is clueless

jokes.text: And we probably shouldn't read anything into the fact that for some reason, Moira decided to hide this from you.
jokes.setup: Johnny defending Moira not telling him
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn pointing out the obvious suspicious nature
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn twisting the knife by pointing out what Johnny is trying to ignore

jokes.text: It's just that I know what it's like to be in bed with a naked Roland Schitt, and I am powerless against that.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Roland sleeping naked
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's TMI about her sexual attraction to naked Roland
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable overshare that makes Johnny's situation worse - perfect cringe comedy

jokes.text: It's called sweat equity, David. I learned about this week.
jokes.setup: Alexis wanting payment for her help
jokes.punchline: Her misapplication of business terminology
jokes.explanation: Alexis trying to sound business-savvy but misunderstanding the concept

jokes.text: I think you might actually need to break a sweat, in order to earn sweat equity.
jokes.setup: Alexis claiming sweat equity for minimal work
jokes.punchline: David's literal interpretation of the term
jokes.explanation: David's dry correction of Alexis's misunderstood business term

jokes.text: Okay, what if I got Patrick to break a sweat for me. That's called outsourcing, outsourcing...
jokes.setup: David's sweat equity correction
jokes.punchline: Alexis doubling down with more misunderstood business terms
jokes.explanation: Alexis's persistence in misapplying business terminology

jokes.text: He's a business major who wears straight leg, mid-range denim. He's not into me.
jokes.setup: Alexis suggesting Patrick might be interested in David
jokes.punchline: David's fashion-based dismissal of romantic possibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - dismissing romantic potential based purely on fashion choices, showing his character's priorities and insecurities

jokes.text: I know, wasn't that funny?
jokes.setup: Johnny confronting Moira about sleeping with Roland
jokes.punchline: Moira treating it as a casual amusing anecdote
jokes.explanation: Moira's casual dismissal of what should be a serious marital issue

jokes.text: Obviously. I didn't tell you anything because I was embarrassed.
jokes.setup: Johnny pointing out they never discussed it
jokes.punchline: Moira finally admitting embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Moira's rare moment of vulnerability and honesty

jokes.text: Moira, that's my beer! You don't even drink beer!
jokes.setup: Moira pouring out alcohol as symbolic gesture
jokes.punchline: Johnny's practical objection to her destroying his beer
jokes.explanation: Johnny's practical frustration undermining Moira's dramatic gesture

jokes.text: It's symbolic, John.
jokes.setup: Johnny objecting to her wasting his beer
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic justification for the waste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira response - treating wasteful gesture as profound symbolism

jokes.text: I um... I actually picked out that frame.
jokes.setup: Patrick claiming he wants to be honest about working together
jokes.punchline: His 'confession' about choosing the frame David criticized
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that reveals Patrick's personality and creates awkward moment with David

jokes.text: I am 99.999% sure that nothing happened between me and your wife.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to reassure Johnny
jokes.punchline: His overly specific percentage that actually sounds less certain
jokes.explanation: The mathematical precision makes Roland sound less confident, not more

jokes.text: I spent 35 minutes this morning inspecting every inch of my body for bite marks, back scratches, lipstick prints.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining his certainty nothing happened
jokes.punchline: His graphic description of self-examination
jokes.explanation: Perfectly uncomfortable overshare that makes Johnny more disturbed, not less

jokes.text: My wife would never go out for a burger when she has a strong penchant for steak.
jokes.setup: Johnny defending Moira's fidelity
jokes.punchline: His food metaphor comparing Roland to a burger and himself to steak
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that's both confidently delivered and subtly insulting to Roland

jokes.text: I had my one chance at a proletarian oasis and I squandered it, passed out Roland a bed.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking about hotel amenities
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic description of missing hotel luxury due to passing out with Roland
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira vocabulary - 'proletarian oasis' for a hotel room shows her dramatic perspective

jokes.text: Do we call him uncle Roland?
jokes.setup: David joking about custody battles
jokes.punchline: Alexis pushing the divorce joke to its logical extreme
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the family joke that makes it more uncomfortable

jokes.text: I just want you to know that no matter what anyone says, you will always be our first dad.
jokes.setup: Family drama about Moira and Roland
jokes.punchline: David's fake-sincere reassurance that implies Johnny could be replaced
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake-sincere line that sounds supportive but actually suggests Johnny's replaceability - perfect David cruelty with a smile
